mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient corporate output that offsets higher financing costs, resulting in positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns specifically during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Higher CPIAUCSL readings correlate with positive 5-day forward returns or yield a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures exceeding a 5 percent year-over-year growth threshold signals overheating consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where RSAFS YoY growth surpasses 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll employment growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 index baseline during a post-shock recovery phase indicates weak manufacturing demand that triggers immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 50% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100 during recovery regimes.
